description_long: This object is a vintage film projector mounted on a tall, four-legged wooden stand. The main body of the projector is a boxy structure made of wood and metal, featuring a pair of large reels and a cylindrical lens assembly on top. Several cables and wires are coiled and draped around the stand, and a leather handle is attached to the upper wooden housing for portability. The front panel includes a dark rectangular section, likely for access or display, and there are visible knobs and dials for manual operation. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with exposed mechanical components and a robust, industrial design typical of early 20th-century projection equipment.
description: A vintage wooden and metal film projector with large reels, mounted on a tall wooden stand and featuring exposed mechanical parts and coiled cables.
description_view_0: Front view showing the reels, lens assembly, front panel, and cables draped over the wooden stand.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the wooden housing, handle, and the side of the projector with visible dials.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the coiled power cable, back panel, and the circular port on the stand.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the reels, side panel, and additional cables hanging from the stand.
attribute_lens_type: fixed; cylindrical lens assembly with no visible adjustment rings
attribute_control_panel_design: Manual control panel with several knobs and dials located on the front and side panels; no digital interface or touch controls visible.
attribute_ventilation_pattern: No visible ventilation pattern; the housing appears mostly solid wood and metal with no obvious vents or grills.
